subject: Databases
title: Database Users and Roles
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Database Users and Roles.

hook.text: Everyone uses the same 'admin' account with full access. One compromised password exposes everything.

buildup.text: Create separate database users for each application and person. Each user gets only the permissions they need.

discovery.text: Roles group permissions. 'readonly' gets SELECT only. 'writer' gets INSERT and UPDATE. Assign roles to users.

twist.text: The app should never connect as superuser. A dedicated app user with limited permissions limits blast radius.

climax.text: GRANT SELECT ON orders TO app_readonly; REVOKE DELETE ON orders FROM app_writer; Be explicit.

punchline.text: Separate users, specific roles. Never share superuser passwords with apps.

quiz.question: Why should applications never connect as the database superuser?
options: A compromised application with superuser access can do unlimited damage to all data
Databases don't allow superuser connections from applications
